message:
fix(block,core,fuse,repair): sequence-aware dirty tracking, VFS/FUSE formatting, and repair refactoring

ARC cache dirty tracking (ffs-block — critical correctness fix):
- Make clear_dirty() sequence-aware: accept a seq parameter and only clear the
  entry if its sequence number matches, preventing a concurrent re-dirty from
  being silently lost when an earlier flush completes
- Add clear_dirty_unconditional() for cases where we always want to remove
  (eviction, invalidation, mark_clean, discard), preserving original behavior
- FlushCandidate now captures the seq at snapshot time; all three flush paths
  (flush_pending_evictions, flush_dirty_batch, flush_dirty) pass it through
- New test dirty_tracker_clear_conditional_prevents_race: T1 marks dirty (seq 0),
  T2 re-dirties (seq 1), T1 flush completes — T2's entry must survive
- New MVCC test file: visibility_race.rs (concurrent read/write stress test)

Core filesystem (ffs-core — 1700+ lines changed):
- Extensive rustfmt formatting pass: break long function signatures, method
  chains, and match arms across multiple lines for readability
- Scope propagation improvements for MVCC isolation in ext4 FsOps methods
- Add public convenience wrappers on OpenFs matching existing pattern
- Remove unused BTRFS_SUPER_MIRROR_SHIFT constant from cmd_repair

VFS trait (ffs-core/vfs.rs):
- Format FsOps trait method signatures to multi-line style
- Simplify RequestScope::commit_and_release error mapping with match expression

FUSE layer (ffs-fuse):
- Format long method call chains in mkdir, write, read, readdir, setxattr,
  removexattr, setattr, unlink handlers for consistency

Journal (ffs-journal):
- Format descriptor_block and commit_block call sites in wrap-around tests

Repair subsystem:
- Add #[must_use] to DurabilityAutopilot::expected_loss_custom
- Refactor exchange Store::get_symbols to use match expression; drop lock
  guard before returning
- Change response_for_lookup and response_for_store return types from
  Result<Response> to plain Response since they cannot fail

Beads: issue tracker updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
